keyPoints: Lightweight DN Label as a multi-signal transparency cue signaling cognitive effort, novelty, and bias within a governance-backed human-in-the-loop framework
Context-sensitive, lightweight label design to minimize cognitive load and avoid overload
Three- to four-arm randomized exposure design to isolate cognition-signal and bias-signal effects on trust, authenticity, and willingness to engage humans
Multidimensional trust, perceived authenticity, willingness to escalate to human oversight, and calibration with AI performance as outcomes
Emphasis on preserving human agency, governance, and data provenance to prevent miscalibration and maintain accountability
Anticipation of mixed effects across domains and user traits; signals should be adaptable with on-demand deeper explanations
Preregister hypotheses and analysis plans; report effect sizes with CIs; ensure ethics and fairness in reporting
